agents.name: Rex
agents.note: Session 97 -- US-225 passes:true. TD-034 CLOSED. All 5 log-only US-216 stage behaviors wired to concrete actions: WARNING sets pi_state.no_new_drives + SyncClient.forcePush; IMMINENT pausePolling + DriveDetector.forceKeyOff(power_imminent); AC-restore clears gate + resumePolling. BT-close on IMMINENT deliberately NOT wired (TRIGGER->systemctl poweroff cascade handles BT teardown via BtResilienceMixin + systemd service-stop; US-211 owns reattach; US-216 invariant 'TRIGGER->poweroff path stays unchanged' takes priority). New: src/pi/obdii/pi_state.py (singleton CHECK(id=1), no_new_drives INT 0/1, ensurePiStateTable idempotent, get/set/clear accessors). DriveDetector.forceKeyOff(reason) reimplements end-drive logic directly (NOT a wrapper around EngineStateMachine -- DriveDetector has its own internal state machine, _lastEngineState is a US-206 metadata tracker not a live SM). Reason threaded _endDrive->_logDriveEvent into existing connection_log.error_message column (no schema change needed); normal debounce drive-end preserved as NULL. _openDriveId gate returns None BEFORE nextDriveId() so drive_counter is NOT incremented when gated. SyncClient.forcePush() + PushSummary(slots=True): wraps pushAllDeltas with explicit logging + aggregated counts; no invariants bypassed (AUTH, retry, failed-HWM-preserve, snapshot-skip all inherited). ApplicationOrchestrator.pausePolling/resumePolling: separate _pollingPausedForPowerDown flag from BT-reconnect's _dataLoggerPausedForReconnect (prevents AC-restore fighting BT reconnect for resume); delegates to RealtimeDataLogger.stop/start; connection untouched; start-raises keeps flag True for retry. lifecycle.py _wirePowerDownOrchestratorCallbacks rewritten with concrete wiring + broad-exception isolation at every step so a raising callback never blocks stage escalation (regression-guarded by test_warningCallbackRaisesButImminentStillFires + test_imminentCallbackRaisesButTriggerStillFires). 5 new test files 51 new tests all passing: test_pi_state_no_new_drives.py (14) + test_force_key_off_api.py (10) + test_sync_force_push.py (9) + test_poll_tier_pause.py (12) + test_orchestrator_stage_behavior_wiring.py (6). specs/architecture.md Section 10.6 (spec said Section 11 but 11 is Deployment; 10.6 is power orchestrator) stage-behavior-wiring block + docs/testing.md drill addendum with 4 shell-command observations + TD-034 CLOSED annotation. Scope path drift from grooming: spec said src/pi/data/database_schema.py + src/pi/data/database.py but actual codebase has them under src/pi/obdii/ -- followed actual paths. Scope-fence honored: did NOT touch US-216 state machine / hysteresis / SOC thresholds / battery_health_log schema / BatteryHealthRecorder / ladder-vs-legacy regression test / US-211 reconnect loop. Also repaired a pre-existing JSON-validity bug in ralph_agents.json itself (Session 96 note had unterminated string -> corrupted file; Session 97 rewrote the whole note cleanly since this is Ralph-owned state). Verification: new 51/51 passed in 78s; fast suite 3172 passed / 17 skipped / 0 regressions in 735s (baseline post-US-223 was 3121, +51 matches exactly); ruff clean on all 11 touched files after --fix (import-sort auto-fixes only); validate_config 4/4 OK; sprint_lint 0 errors / 18 pre-existing sizing warnings (US-225 flags sized M but filesToTouch=16 consider L -- 16 is the scope filesToTouch list which includes path-corrected originals, accepted). Sprint 17 status after US-225: 6/6 passes:true -- Sprint 17 complete, ready for PM close-out + merge.
